title: Unit tests — CredentialsStore defaults and Source decoding
description: Implement AggregatorAppTests.swift with three tests. (1) testCredentialsStoreDefaults: construct CredentialsStore via its test initialiser (in-memory backing); assert baseURL == 'https://aggregator-api.renaliaslabs.net/api/v1', clientId == '', clientSecret == '', isConfigured == false. (2) testCredentialsStoreIsConfigured: set all three to non-empty strings; assert isConfigured == true. (3) testSourceDecodingFromJSON: decode '{"id":1,"name":"Test Feed","feed_url":"https://example.com/feed.xml"}' into Source; assert id == 1, name == 'Test Feed', feedURL == 'https://example.com/feed.xml'. No test may access the real Keychain or UserDefaults.
acceptance_criteria: testCredentialsStoreDefaults passes with the expected default values
testCredentialsStoreIsConfigured passes: all three non-empty strings yields isConfigured == true
testSourceDecodingFromJSON passes: all three fields decode correctly including the feed_url → feedURL mapping
No test accesses the real Keychain or real UserDefaults (uses injectable in-memory backing from CredentialsStore)
All three tests pass under xcodebuild test
